name: Mobile Phase 6 — Hardening + Policies
outcome_goal: Stolen-phone hardening lands (step-up freshness <30s, per-device revoke CLI, visible burn-rate ceiling). Four end-to-end chain-attack tests prove the authority path cannot be bypassed. Blast-radius thresholds are halved concurrent with mobile ship (moral-hazard correction from the debate). Five architecture policies are filed into operator_decisions. A hostile review doc names every remaining autorun_ok or _SAFE_AUTO_EXECUTE_TYPES reference — or confirms zero.
anti_requirements: Do NOT introduce new tables for hardening — all three defenses are thin additions to existing code.
Do NOT relax blast-radius thresholds anywhere else to compensate for the halve.
Do NOT ship the review doc with any BLOCKER finding unresolved.
Do NOT widen writes outside handlers/, cli/, surfaces/app/src/mobile/components/, docs/architecture/, and one migration.
Do NOT skip test 3 (in-scope blast-radius gate) — it is the pillar-4 correction.
notes: Four jobs lifted from config/cascade/specs/W_mobile_access_plan_20260420.queue.json wave 6. One new job (wave6_blast_radius_threshold_halve) files migration 199 with halved defaults: 25 files / 2.5 MiB / 10 external calls / $5. Roadmap policies extended to five — adds arch_session_blast_radius_gate and arch_policy_drift_inverse_check per the debate synthesis.

jobs.label: wave6_stolen_phone_defense
jobs.description: Harden against stolen-unlocked-phone: step-up freshness, per-device revoke CLI, visible burn-rate ceiling.
jobs.outcome_goal: A stolen unlocked phone cannot ratify a new approval after 30s without re-biometric, cannot exceed the active grant budget, and can be revoked in one CLI command from Nate's desktop.
jobs.prompt:
Three specific defenses:

1. Step-up freshness: in POST /approvals/{id}/ratify, reject if step_up_challenge.created_at > 30s ago; verified in tests.

2. Per-device revoke: add 'praxis workflow capability revoke-device --label <label>' CLI that marks device_enrollments.revoked_at=now(), invalidates all mobile_sessions for that enrollment, and revokes all active capability_grants with device_enrollment_id=<enrollment>. Write CLI + integration test at tests/integration/test_device_revoke.py.

3. Visible budget burn-rate: BurnRateHeader.tsx already shows 'Burn rate: $X / $Y'; add hard visual change when burn_rate_cents_per_hour projects to exceed budget_ceiling before expires_at (panel turns red-border + burn rate in red). Human-visible signal that autonomy is running hot.

Do not invent new tables. All three defenses are thin additions to existing code.

jobs.label: wave6_e2e_chain_attack
jobs.description: End-to-end tests for the chain attacks the debate identified: scope-widen, remediation-bypass, in-scope blast-radius, and policy-drift.
jobs.outcome_goal: Four E2E integration tests prove the attack paths are stopped: (a) workflow edits a file outside its grant's write_scope -> gate; (b) workflow attempts FIX_AND_RETRY without a covering grant -> approval_request opened; (c) in-scope plan crossing session_blast_radius_policy threshold -> gate regardless of grant coverage; (d) runtime attempting api.anthropic.com call emits a policy_drift_events row and surfaces on mobile metrics.
jobs.prompt:
Write end-to-end integration tests at Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/integration/test_chain_attack_defense.py.

Test 1 — commit-plus-push chain:
- Enroll fixture device.
- Issue grant covering {allowed_operations: ['workflow.submit', 'git.add', 'git.commit'], write_scope: ['docs/']}. NOT git.push, NOT Code&DBs/.
- Simulate intent sequence: edit docs/foo.md (covered), git.commit (covered), edit Code&DBs/runtime/control_commands.py (NOT covered — write_scope mismatch), assert classify_control_intent returns GATE with reason_code='write_scope_outside_grant'.
- Assert approval_requests row with proposed_plan_envelope including Code&DBs/ in write_scope.

Test 2 — migration-plus-restart chain:
- Grant covering ['data_dictionary.read_schema', 'data_dictionary.run_local_tests'].
- Simulate data_dictionary_governance_remediation emitting 'sync.repair' (not covered).
- Assert grant_ref=None + open_approval_request triggered.
- Assert self_healing FIX_AND_RETRY similarly gated to 'pending_approval'.

Test 3 — in-scope blast-radius (pillar 4):
- Grant covering ['workflow.submit', 'fs.write'] with write_scope ['Code&DBs/Workflow/'] and budget_ceiling_cents 10000.
- UPDATE session_blast_radius_policy SET threshold_files_mutated=5 WHERE policy_id='default' (tighten for test).
- Open session, bump record_mutation six times.
- Assert 6th call triggers gate: gate_triggered_at non-null, gate_triggered_dim='files_mutated'.
- Assert approval_requests row with intent_kind='blast_radius_threshold_crossed' + gate_approval_ref linked.
- Assert gate fires DESPITE active grant + unexhausted budget — orthogonal to grant coverage.

Test 4 — policy drift surfaced (pillar 3):
- Monkey-patch task_assembler._call_haiku's provider string back to 'anthropic'.
- Invoke the call; assert it raises.
- Assert exactly one new policy_drift_events row with policy_id='providers::anthropic', detector_kind='anthropic_api_call_attempt'.
- Call policy_drift.recent_drift_count(conn, 24); assert >= 1.
- Call mobile_metrics.snapshot with fixture conn; assert response['quality']['policy_drift_count_24h'] >= 1.

All four tests use rollback-isolated conns.

jobs.label: wave6_blast_radius_threshold_halve
jobs.description: Migration 199: halve blast-radius thresholds concurrent with mobile ship. Debate-mandated moral-hazard correction.
jobs.outcome_goal: session_blast_radius_policy row 'default' now has threshold_files_mutated=25, threshold_bytes_written=2621440 (2.5 MiB), threshold_external_calls=10, threshold_spend_cents=500 ($5). A decision row records the halve with the debate decision_ref.
jobs.prompt:
Write Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/199_blast_radius_policy_halve.sql:

BEGIN;

UPDATE session_blast_radius_policy
SET threshold_files_mutated   = 25,
    threshold_bytes_written   = 2621440,
    threshold_external_calls  = 10,
    threshold_spend_cents     = 500,
    decision_ref              = 'decision.2026-04-23.blast-radius-mobile-moral-hazard-halve',
    rationale                 = 'Halved concurrent with mobile oversight ship. Debate debate_mobile_access_architecture_20260420 identified that mobile surface creates moral hazard — Nate approves more easily from the phone, so per-session ceilings must tighten to compensate. Any dimension crossing still triggers mid-plan approval; halving shifts where that line is drawn by 2x. Revisit after 14 days of behavior data.',
    updated_at                = now()
WHERE policy_id = 'default';

-- File the decision row explicitly so operator_decisions authority has the trace.
INSERT INTO operator_decisions (
    policy_id,
    scope_ref,
    title,
    rationale,
    status,
    decided_at
) VALUES (
    'decision.2026-04-23.blast-radius-mobile-moral-hazard-halve',
    'session_blast_radius_policy::default',
    'Blast-radius thresholds halved concurrent with mobile oversight ship',
    'Mobile oversight creates a moral-hazard vector: approvals become easier from the phone, so per-session working-set ceilings must tighten to compensate. Halved: files 50->25, bytes 5MiB->2.5MiB, external_calls 20->10, spend $10->$5. Revisit 2026-05-07 with 14 days of data.',
    'active',
    now()
) ON CONFLICT (policy_id) DO NOTHING;

COMMIT;

-- Verification:
--   SELECT threshold_files_mutated, threshold_bytes_written, threshold_external_calls, threshold_spend_cents FROM session_blast_radius_policy WHERE policy_id='default';
--   Expected: 25, 2621440, 10, 500

Register in Code&DBs/Workflow/system_authority/workflow_migration_authority.json. Regenerate the Python authority artifact.
jobs.verify_command: psql postgresql://localhost:5432/praxis -f 'Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/199_blast_radius_policy_halve.sql' && psql postgresql://localhost:5432/praxis -c "SELECT threshold_files_mutated, threshold_bytes_written, threshold_external_calls, threshold_spend_cents FROM session_blast_radius_policy WHERE policy_id='default'" | grep -q '25 | 2621440 | 10 | 500'

jobs.label: wave6_roadmap_policies
jobs.description: File the five architecture policies the synthesis produced into Praxis.db operator_decisions.
jobs.outcome_goal: Five rows exist in operator_decisions with the policy-ids arch_mobile_pwa_webauthn, arch_authorization_capability_ledger, arch_minimalist_mobile_metrics, arch_session_blast_radius_gate, arch_policy_drift_inverse_check, each citing this queue_id as decision source.
jobs.prompt:
Call praxis_operator_architecture_policy to file five rows.

1. { policy_id: 'arch_mobile_pwa_webauthn', title: 'Mobile Control Surface must be a WebAuthn-backed Thin PWA', rationale: 'Native apps add App-Store deployment latency incompatible with zero-day remediation windows against autonomous threats. Bespoke cryptography (custom ed25519 HTTP signatures) introduces canonicalization ambiguities that an autonomous agent will exploit. Passkey + PWA solves hardware attestation and instant deploy simultaneously. Source: debate workflow_bf339d4a5629 + continuation workflow_e2f4e3d241b1.', status: 'active' }.

2. { policy_id: 'arch_authorization_capability_ledger', title: 'Eradicate Label-Per-Action Authz in favor of Capability Grants', rationale: 'Autonomous agents bypass static verb-level authorization (_SAFE_AUTO_EXECUTE_TYPES) by chaining benign actions into destructive payloads. All auto-execution must be validated against a stateful, budget-aware capability ledger (capability_grants) covering the whole plan envelope. The existing gate_evaluations table is extended with grant_ref + plan_envelope_hash, not replaced.', status: 'active' }.

3. { policy_id: 'arch_minimalist_mobile_metrics', title: 'Mobile metrics restricted to Pending Approvals + Burn Rate header and five compact panels', rationale: 'The mobile surface is a capability control gate, not a dashboard. The always-visible header shows only Pending Approvals, Burn Rate, and active-session blast-radius; the five panels (Flow, Economy, Providers, Quality, System) are compact-idle and expand on tap. No chart, no sparkline, no scrolling inside a panel. Collapse and simplify.', status: 'active' }.

4. { policy_id: 'arch_session_blast_radius_gate', title: 'Session working-set gates fire regardless of grant coverage', rationale: 'Per-action risk tags (_SAFE_AUTO_EXECUTE_TYPES) and per-plan grants both miss the damage shape the plan approves in aggregate but would not have approved at size. A session-scoped blast-radius counter (files_mutated, bytes_written, external_calls, spend_cents) crossed against session_blast_radius_policy fires a mid-plan approval_request independent of grant coverage. Source: debate debate_mobile_access_architecture_20260420 pillar 4.', status: 'active' }.

5. { policy_id: 'arch_policy_drift_inverse_check', title: 'operator_decisions is round-tripped: runtime emits drift on violation', rationale: 'A static authority row only protects behavior if the runtime is instrumented to honor it. The inverse check — whenever runtime hits a code path the authority declared inactive — emits a policy_drift_events row. First detector covers anthropic CLI-only enforcement across five neutralized paths. Future policies register detectors via runtime.capability.policy_drift.emit_drift. policy_drift_count_24h is the headline oversight metric. Source: debate debate_mobile_access_architecture_20260420 pillar 3.', status: 'active' }.

Verify by querying operator_decisions for each policy_id.
jobs.verify_command: psql postgresql://localhost:5432/praxis -c "SELECT policy_id FROM operator_decisions WHERE policy_id IN ('arch_mobile_pwa_webauthn', 'arch_authorization_capability_ledger', 'arch_minimalist_mobile_metrics', 'arch_session_blast_radius_gate', 'arch_policy_drift_inverse_check')" | grep -c arch_ | grep -q 5

jobs.label: wave6_review
jobs.description: Hostile code review of the whole integration against the threat model.
jobs.outcome_goal: A written review at docs/architecture/mobile_access_plan_review.md names every place the capability ledger could be bypassed, every remaining autorun_ok or _SAFE_AUTO_EXECUTE_TYPES reference, every test gap, and either closes each finding with a linked fix commit or files a bug.
jobs.prompt:
Hostile review. Pretend you are the autonomous builder trying to chain around the new controls.

Must check:
1. grep -r '_SAFE_AUTO_EXECUTE_TYPES\|autorun_ok' Code&DBs/Workflow/ returns zero matches in non-test, non-archive paths.
2. Every site that constructs a ControlIntent passes plan_hash before classify_control_intent.
3. Every path that writes gate_evaluations includes grant_ref + plan_envelope_hash when a grant is active.
4. self_healing.py, data_dictionary_governance_remediation.py, canonical_workflows.py, workflow_chain.py, command_handlers.py, chat_tools.py all route recovery/remediation/retry intents through resolve_capability_grant.
5. POST /approvals/{id}/ratify rejects stale step-up assertions (>30s).
6. Revoking a device revokes its sessions AND its active grants atomically.
7. Budget accounting uses atomic UPDATE with CHECK constraint; two concurrent consumes cannot both succeed.
8. PWA service-worker does not cache any API response (only app shell).
9. Cookie is httpOnly + SameSite=Strict + Secure in production.
10. No plaintext bootstrap tokens persist in Praxis.db — only hashed, with expires_at.
11. WebAuthn RP ID is validated server-side, not trusted from client.
12. The chain-attack E2E tests at tests/integration/test_chain_attack_defense.py actually fail if the authority path is disabled (negative validation).

Produce docs/architecture/mobile_access_plan_review.md with findings grouped: BLOCKERS, MAJOR, MINOR, NITS. For each finding, a proposed fix and file:line. If clean, say so.
